content.problemStatement:
Dr. Hasan is one of the most famous inventors in the world. One day, he invented a wonderful machine in order to examine the possibility of generating new palindrome strings by merging other strings.

Hasan's machine takes a sequence of strings (A1,A2,…,AK)(A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_K)(A1​,A2​,…,AK​) as the input; KKK may be any positive integer. The length of each of these strings must be exactly NNN; for each valid jjj, let's denote the jjj-th character of the iii-th string by Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j​. The machine magically generates a string BBB by merging these strings and it determines if BBB is a palindrome. The string BBB is generated as follows:


B1=A1,1,B2=A2,1,…,BK=AK,1B_1 = A_{1, 1}, B_2 = A_{2, 1}, \ldots, B_K = A_{K, 1}B1​=A1,1​,B2​=A2,1​,…,BK​=AK,1​
BK+1=A1,2,BK+2=A2,2,…,B2⋅K=AK,2B_{K+1} = A_{1, 2}, B_{K+2} = A_{2, 2}, \ldots, B_{2 \cdot K} = A_{K, 2}BK+1​=A1,2​,BK+2​=A2,2​,…,B2⋅K​=AK,2​
...
B(N−1)⋅K+1=A1,N,B(N−1)⋅K+2=A2,N,…,BN⋅K=AK,NB_{(N-1) \cdot K + 1} = A_{1, N}, B_{(N-1) \cdot K + 2} = A_{2, N}, \ldots, B_{N \cdot K} = A_{K, N}B(N−1)⋅K+1​=A1,N​,B(N−1)⋅K+2​=A2,N​,…,BN⋅K​=AK,N​
Hasan wants to test his new machine, so he prepared NNN strings S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​. Each of these strings has length NNN. Now, he wants to choose a sequence of KKK pairwise distinct indices i1,i2,…,iKi_1, i_2, \ldots, i_Ki1​,i2​,…,iK​, where 1≤K≤M1 \le K \le M1≤K≤M, and run the machine with strings (Si1,Si2,…,SiK)(S_{i_1}, S_{i_2}, \ldots, S_{i_K})(Si1​​,Si2​​,…,SiK​​). His goal is to make the machine report that the string it generated is a palindrome.

Can you help Hasan with testing his machine and tell him the number of sequences of indices he can choose? Since the answer could be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​ contain only lowercase English letters
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1035 \cdot 10^35⋅103
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and  MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th of these lines contains a single string SiS_iSi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of sequences Hasan can choose, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: Hasan can choose the following sequences of indices:

(1)(1)(1): the input of the machine is ("aaaa") and the generated string BBB is "aaaa"
(2,4)(2, 4)(2,4): the input is ("abcb", "bcba") and BBB is "abbccbba"
(3,4)(3, 4)(3,4): the input is ("abcb", "bcba") and BBB is "abbccbba"
(4,2)(4, 2)(4,2): the input is ("bcba", "abcb") and BBB is "bacbbcab"
(4,3)(4, 3)(4,3): the input is ("bcba", "abcb") and BBB is "bacbbcab"
